channel.title: Bay Runner Charters, Bay Notes
channel.description: Season updates and fishing reports from Captain Jack Singleton aboard the F/V Bay Runner, Annapolis, Maryland.

item.title: Fall rockfish are on the structure
item.description: The fall fish have fattened up and moved onto the structure. Cool, breezy, falling water temperature, and the October windows are fishing exactly the way they should. These are the best eating fish of the season. A handful of dates are still open before the boat hauls out in December.

item.title: Last call before the boat comes out
item.description: Commercial crab season closes at the end of November and the Bay Runner hauls out at Marshwood Partners in mid-December for the winter. If you have been meaning to book a fall rockfish trip, now is the week. Half day is $675 and a full day is $1,150, gear and bait provided, catch cleaned at the dock.

item.title: How summer 2026 fished
item.description: The full days outfished the half days again this summer, and the morning trips beat the midday ones once the August heat set in. Repeat parties booked the same windows they always do. The lesson holds, on a slack hot morning you keep moving until you find the birds working, you do not sit.

item.title: Bringing kids aboard the Bay Runner
item.description: This is a family friendly boat and a half day is the right length for younger anglers. Late summer bluefish give nonstop action, there is a head aboard, and light tackle so a small angler can actually fight a fish. My own kids learned to fish off this deck.

item.title: What a fall rockfish day looks like
item.description: Cool, breezy, falling water temperature, and the fish were fat and holding on the structure. We limited out on quality rockfish by early afternoon. This is exactly why the October windows are worth holding open even though it is past the main summer slate.

item.title: Booking, deposits, and weather calls
item.description: A deposit holds your date and the balance is due on the dock, cash or card. Bookings run through Pete Andersen at Chesapeake Charter Connection. If the captain cancels for weather the trip reschedules at no charge. We fish rain or shine, lightning and small craft advisories permitting.
